Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS)
Multi-extortion model

Highly industrialized ransomware operations. Annual damage forecast hits $74B in 2026. Business struck every 2 seconds. Growing multi-extortion: encrypt + exfiltrate + DDoS + notify customers.

📅 Regulatory Calendar 2026

Upcoming cybersecurity and crypto regulations, compliance deadlines, and enforcement milestones. Click Refresh for live updates.

Q2 2026
Apr 15
EU DORA — Full Enforcement
Digital Operational Resilience Act now fully enforceable. ICT risk management, incident reporting, and third-party risk requirements for all EU financial entities.
EUFinServMandatory
May 1
SEC Cyber Incident Reporting — Annual Review
SEC review of material cyber incident disclosure compliance. Companies must demonstrate 4-day reporting capability.
USPublic Co
Jun 1
MiCA Phase 2 — CASPs Full Scope
Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation expands to all crypto-asset service providers. AML/KYC requirements for all exchanges operating in the EU.
EUCryptoAML
Q3 2026
Jul 1
NIS2 Enforcement Deadline — Extended
EU member states must have NIS2 transposed into national law. Mandatory incident reporting within 24 hours for essential and important entities.
EUCritical InfraMandatory
Aug 15
CIRCIA — CISA Incident Reporting Rules
Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act rules expected to finalize. 72-hour incident and 24-hour ransomware payment reporting.
USCritical InfraRansomware
Sep 30
APAC — Singapore MAS Technology Risk Update
Updated MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines with expanded third-party and cloud security requirements.
APACFinServ
Q4 2026
Oct 1
NYDFS Cybersecurity Amendment — Final Phase
Final phase of NYDFS 23 NYCRR 500 amendments. Enhanced governance, access controls, and monitoring requirements for regulated financial institutions.
USFinServNY State
Dec 31
Global Travel Rule — FATF Compliance Review
FATF mutual evaluation round. Countries assessed on Travel Rule implementation for virtual asset service providers.
GlobalCryptoFATF

⚔️
Maltego — Competitor (See Battlecards tab)
Maltego is an OSINT graph investigation platform and direct competitor to Orion in the law enforcement market. Not a data partner.
⚡ Orion Use Cases 18 USE CASES · BUYER-PERSONA · INVESTIGATION FLOWS · PROOF POINTS
Orion is an AI-native criminal investigation platform — NOT a threat monitoring or SOC tool. Primary buyers: law enforcement, financial crime teams, crypto exchanges. Core value: replacing 40-hour manual investigations with 2-hour AI-automated ones.
🔒
Ransomware Attribution & Prosecution
LockBit · ALPHV · BlackBasta · REvil
🏛️ LE

Trace ransom payments from victim wallets through mixers, chain hops, and exchange cashouts to identify and attribute threat actors. Generate court-admissible chain-of-custody reports. Before Orion: 40–80 hours per case. With Orion: 2–4 hours.

Investigation Flow
Victim wallet → NLQ trace through Tornado Cash/JoinMarket → Cross-chain bridge detection → Exchange attribution → Threat actor entity graph → SAR/court package
Proof
FBI CCIU uses Orion for ransomware attribution. NCA reduced investigation time from 3 weeks to 2 days. IRS-CI: 85% time reduction on crypto investigations.
TRM BlockchainFlashpointChainabuseVirusTotal
🇰🇵
DPRK / Lazarus Group Financial Attribution
North Korea Crypto Heists · Sanctions Enforcement
🏛️ LE · 🛡️ NatSec

Trace Lazarus Group crypto heists (Ronin $625M, Harmony $100M, Bybit $1.4B) through complex mixer sequences to identify cashout points. Supports OFAC designations and LE interdiction. Orion has pre-built Lazarus attribution models.

Investigation Flow
Exchange hack wallet → Automated mixer trace (Tornado, Railgun, cross-chain) → Entity graph → Lazarus TTPs fingerprint match → OFAC SDN referral package → Treasury coordination
Proof
NSA/CISA joint advisories cite TRM methodology. US Treasury OFAC uses TRM entity data for designation decisions. $3B+ Lazarus funds tracked via TRM.
TRM Entity GraphOFAC SanctionsBlockchain NodeExa
🌑
Dark Web Criminal Marketplace Investigation
Drugs · Weapons · Fraud · CSAM Financial Tracing
🏛️ LE

Identify and attribute dark web marketplace operators by fusing on-chain payment data with forum personas, PGP keys, and OSINT. Map the financial infrastructure behind dark web operations. CSAM financial tracing for victim identification and prosecution.

Investigation Flow
Dark web market wallet → Payment pattern analysis → Vendor wallet clustering → Forum persona correlation (PGP/handle) → OSINT identity resolution → Warrant package
Proof
Europol Operation SpecTor used TRM data — largest dark web enforcement 2023. DEA, HSI active Orion deployments for dark web narcotics finance.
FlashpointWebz.ioFirecrawlPiplTRM Blockchain
💣
Terrorist Financing & Sanctions Evasion
ISIS · Hamas · Hezbollah · Iran IRGC · Russia
🏛️ LE · 🛡️ NatSec

Map crypto funding networks for designated terrorist organisations. Trace sanctions evasion schemes routing through TRON, Monero, and hawala networks. Support OFAC designation evidence packages and LE interdiction operations.

Investigation Flow
Known terrorist wallet → Funding network mapping → Donor wallet attribution → Exchange KYC data request coordination → OFAC referral → Treasury FINCEN CTR filing
Proof
FBI Counter-Terrorism Division, USSS, and Five Eyes CT units use Orion for crypto terrorism finance investigations.
TRM SanctionsPiplOpenCorporatesExa
📋
SAR / STR Investigation & Auto-Generation
FinCEN · NCA · AUSTRAC · FCA
🏦 FinServ · 🔄 Exchange

Orion investigates flagged AML transactions end-to-end — from SAR trigger to threat actor attribution — and auto-generates the SAR narrative with full chain-of-custody. Reduces SAR filing time from 4 hours to 20 minutes.

Investigation Flow
TMS alert → Orion NLQ investigation → Blockchain trace + dark web context → Entity attribution → SAR narrative auto-draft → Compliance officer review → FinCEN filing
Proof
JPMorgan Chase, HSBC, and Barclays use TRM for crypto AML investigation. FinCEN cites TRM tracing methodology in public guidance. Santander and Standard Chartered active engagements.
TRM BlockchainDow JonesComplyAdvantageSpyCloud
🏦
Ransomware Response & SEC Cyber Disclosure
SEC 4-Day Disclosure · Incident Investigation · IR Support
🏦 FinServ

When a financial institution is hit by ransomware, Orion provides the investigation evidence required for SEC 8-K cyber disclosure within the 4-day window. Attributes threat actor, traces ransom payment, and generates forensic evidence package for legal and regulatory filing.

Investigation Flow
Ransom demand received → Orion traces payment wallets → Threat actor attribution (LockBit/ALPHV/etc) → SEC 8-K evidence package → Regulatory disclosure → LE referral coordination
ROI
SEC cyber disclosure liability requires documented investigation. Orion provides the evidence trail that satisfies SEC, reduces legal exposure, and supports FBI/CISA coordination.
TRM BlockchainRecorded FutureFlashpointCensys
🕵️
Beneficial Owner Investigation (EDD)
Shell Companies · PEP Screening · KYB
🏦 FinServ

Link crypto wallets to real beneficial owners through OSINT, corporate registry, and identity data fusion. Critical for Enhanced Due Diligence on high-risk customers and correspondent banking relationships. Identifies shell company structures used in DPRK and Russian sanctions evasion.

Investigation Flow
High-risk account flagged → Wallet risk screening → OSINT identity resolution → Corporate registry trace (BVI/Cayman/WY) → PEP/sanctions check → EDD report → Relationship decision
Proof
Binance used TRM-powered EDD to satisfy FinCEN DOJ settlement requirements. HSBC active engagement for correspondent banking due diligence.
OpenCorporatesPiplRefinitivLexisNexisDow Jones
🔄
Real-Time Transaction Screening
OFAC · Sanctions · Risk Scoring · Pre-Confirmation
🔄 Exchange

Screen every deposit and withdrawal in real time against OFAC SDN, darknet markets, mixer exposure, and ransomware wallets. Sub-200ms response time. Alerts in the mempool before transaction confirms — stop sanctioned funds before they land.

Investigation Flow
Deposit initiated → Mempool detection → Real-time risk score (sub-200ms) → OFAC SDN match check → Auto-block or flag → Compliance queue → SAR filing if needed
ROI
Binance, Coinbase, and Kraken use TRM. Binance avoided $4.3B+ in additional fines post-DOJ settlement by deploying Orion-level investigation capability. 1 OFAC violation fine averages $150M–$1B.
TRM SanctionsTRM Entity DBBlockchain Node
🧳
Travel Rule Compliance & VASP Due Diligence
MiCA · FATF · FinCEN · AUSTRAC
🔄 Exchange · 🏦 FinServ

Screen VASP counterparties for AML risk before VASP-to-VASP transfers. Satisfy Travel Rule obligations (FATF, MiCA, FinCEN) by combining TRM wallet risk with Dow Jones PEP/sanctions screening on beneficial owners. Critical for EU MiCA compliance deadline.

Investigation Flow
Outbound transfer initiated → Counterparty VASP wallet screening → Beneficial owner PEP/sanctions check → Risk threshold decision → Transfer proceed/block/enhanced review → Audit trail
ROI
MiCA Travel Rule enforcement began 2024. Non-compliance: up to 10% of annual revenue. Orion provides the counterparty screening capability MiCA requires.
TRM Wallet ScreeningComplyAdvantageRefinitiv
🐷
Pig Butchering & Crypto Fraud Investigation
Sha Zhu Pan · Romance Scams · Fraud Recovery
🔄 Exchange · 🏛️ LE

Cluster thousands of pig butchering victim reports by shared fraud infrastructure. Identify the criminal organisation behind multiple fraud operations, trace funds to cashout points, and generate LE referral packages for international coordination. $75B+ industry — Orion's highest-volume LE use case in APAC.

Investigation Flow
Victim reports (Chainabuse/NCFTA) → Fraud wallet clustering → Infrastructure correlation → Criminal org mapping → Exchange account attribution → MLA request package → Recovery coordination
Proof
Binance REACT team uses TRM for pig butchering investigations. Coinbase active engagement. AFP and Singapore Police Force use Orion for APAC fraud tracing.
ChainabusePiplWebz.ioTRM Blockchain
🛡️

🛡️ NatSec

Map adversary crypto infrastructure to identify nation-state operational funding, sanctions evasion networks, and cyber operation financing. Fuse blockchain intelligence with SIGINT and HUMINT for targeting packages. All Five Eyes agencies use TRM for this mission.

⚖️
VASP Examination & Supervisory Investigation
FinCEN · FCA · AUSTRAC · MAS · BaFin
⚖️ Regulator

Regulators use Orion to conduct crypto examination deep-dives on VASPs during supervision cycles. Assess VASP risk exposure, verify SAR quality, and investigate specific transaction patterns. Reduces VASP examination from 40+ hours to under 4 hours.

Investigation Flow
Examination initiated → VASP transaction sample → Orion risk exposure analysis → SAR quality review → Pattern anomaly detection → Examination findings report → Enforcement referral if needed
Proof
FinCEN uses TRM data for VASP enforcement. AUSTRAC active TRM engagement. MAS uses TRM for Singapore VASP supervision. EU AMLA procurement in pipeline.
TRM BlockchainTRM SanctionsComplyAdvantage
🔗
Cross-Chain & Cross-Border Fund Tracing
40+ Chains · Bridge Detection · Mixer Traversal
🏛️ LE · 🏦 FinServ · 🛡️ NatSec

Automatically trace funds across 40+ blockchains (BTC → ETH → TRON → SOL → XMR) without losing the trail at bridges or chain hops. Detects mixer usage and reconstructs the flow. Key differentiator: Chainalysis stops at the chain boundary. Orion traces through.

Investigation Flow
Source wallet (any chain) → NLQ trace request → Automated bridge/hop detection → Cross-chain entity resolution → End destination attribution → Report generation
Key Question
"Can you trace through Tornado Cash and then across to TRON?" — Yes. This is the single most common gap with Chainalysis Reactor. Ask prospects to demonstrate this in their current tool.
TRM BlockchainTRM Entity GraphBlockchain Node
⚔️ Competitive Battlecards Notion-Sourced Intelligence · Updated Apr 2026
These are Orion competitors — investigation & intelligence platforms positioned against Orion in the cyber investigation market.
Palantir Gotham / AIP
A powerful general-purpose data platform dominant in defense and intelligence — Orion wins when buyers need purpose-built investigative intelligence with rapid time-to-value, crypto-native attribution, and analyst-friendly AI.
US · UK · EMEA Federal Intel · DoD · Enterprise
PRIMARY COMPETITOR
✅
Why Orion Wins
• Purpose-built for crypto/cyber/financial crime — Palantir requires 6-18 months of engineering
• Time to value: Orion delivers in hours; Palantir in quarters
• Native blockchain intelligence — no custom connectors needed
• Cost: Palantir $10M+ annually; Orion at a fraction
• Natural language UX — no data engineers or Ontology setup
• Operationally agile vs. strategic infrastructure
⚠️
Why Orion Loses
• Palantir's brand in defense/intel is unmatched (JSOC, CIA, DHS, FBI)
• Handles any data type and workflow — Orion is narrower
• Existing multi-year gov contracts and FedRAMP/IL5 approvals
• AIP for Defense is a DoD priority
• Scale: full-enterprise data infrastructure vs. investigative tool
🛡️
Objection Handling
"We already have Palantir"
Palantir is a data platform, not an investigation tool. Orion is what your analysts use on day one — no Ontology configuration needed. Orion complements Palantir where it exists.
"Palantir's AI can do the same thing"
Palantir AIP requires engineers to configure pipelines. TRM's blockchain intel and Orion's investigation graph took years to build — Palantir would need a multi-year custom build at 10x the cost.
🎣
Trap Questions
• From new use case ID to first analyst query in Palantir — how long and how many engineers?
• Can analysts trace ransomware payments through a mixer across 3 chains today without an engineering request?
• Has Palantir's blockchain attribution been used as primary court evidence?
• Total Palantir implementation cost including SI contract — cost per investigation?
Days
Time-to-first-investigation vs. months for Palantir custom build
10-50x
Orion cost advantage for investigative use case vs. Palantir TCO
0
Native blockchain attribution in Palantir — requires custom connector
IBM i2 Analyst's Notebook
A trusted legacy tool for structured intelligence and link analysis — Orion wins when buyers need AI-native OSINT, conversational investigation, and modern analyst UX. "Orion is to i2 as Figma was to Adobe Illustrator."
Global · UK · Commonwealth · NATO LE · Military Intel · Fraud
HIGH FREQUENCY
✅
Why Orion Wins
• AI-native and conversational — i2 requires manual link chart building
• OSINT-centric: dark web, social media, real-time feeds — i2 needs structured DB inputs
• Instant value delivery: insight in 30-min demo vs. weeks of training
• Bundled FDOS/analyst support model
• Blockchain intelligence is native — no third-party connectors
• "Land now, expand later" — i2 integration gets TRM into agencies fast
⚠️
Why Orion Loses
• Decades of institutional presence — "i2 training" is a career requirement for many analysts
• Deep NATO and Five Eyes adoption — procurement comfort
• Handles telecoms metadata, financial records, arrest records extremely well
• Large ecosystem of trained analysts with built workflows
• IBM enterprise contracts reduce procurement friction
🛡️
Objection Handling
"Our analysts all know i2 — switching costs too much"
Training on i2 is a sunk cost. Orion onboarding for i2-trained analysts takes hours, not months. Plus, TRM data plugs directly into i2 — add Orion on top, don't replace.
"i2 is on our approved vendor list"
TRM has navigated FedRAMP, IL-4/5, NATO STANAG environments. Orion has been procured by national LE agencies across US, UK, Europe, APAC, and LatAm.
🎣
Trap Questions
• From crypto wallet to complete attribution graph in i2 — what manual steps, how long each?
• Can i2 search dark web + breached creds + blockchain + social in one query?
• Last new data source added to i2 — timeline from "want" to "analyst can use it"?
• When i2 produces a ransomware link chart — what blockchain data is in it?
AI-native
Conversational interface vs. manual diagram building
Partner
TRM BLOCKINT feeds into i2 — land now, expand to Orion
Hours
Orion onboarding for i2-trained analysts vs. weeks for i2 setup
Maltego
A powerful OSINT graph tool with 300+ data transforms and a strong analyst community — Orion wins when buyers need automated AI investigation, crypto-native attribution, and rapid workflows rather than manual pivot-by-pivot graph building.
Global · EMEA · Americas Cybercrime Investigators · OSINT · Fraud Teams
BEATABLE
✅
Why Orion Wins
• Automates entire investigations that Maltego requires manual transform-by-transform building
• Native blockchain intelligence — orders of magnitude deeper than Maltego's transforms
• Conversational AI generates summaries and reports automatically
• Multi-chain crypto natively — Maltego has no cross-chain tracing
• Browser-based, zero-install — Maltego requires desktop setup
• Reasons across cyber + crypto + identity simultaneously
⚠️
Why Orion Loses
• 300+ transform library (Shodan, DomainTools, VirusTotal) — unmatched OSINT breadth
• Strong analyst community with years of workflows and certifications
• Lower cost — Community free; Enterprise $15K-$50K
• Visual graph output is "show your work" — preferred for court submissions
• Conservative agencies prefer non-AI, explainable output
🛡️
Objection Handling
"Maltego connects to the same sources"
Maltego connects your analysts to data. Orion connects the data for your analysts — automatically, in parallel, with AI reasoning. Watch Orion do in a single query what takes dozens of manual Maltego pivots.
"Our team invested in Maltego training"
Orion is additive, not a rip-and-replace. When a crypto wallet comes in as a lead — how deep can Maltego trace across chains, through bridges, into mixers? Orion picks up where Maltego stops.
🎣
Trap Questions
• How many Maltego transforms for a complete ransomware investigation? Average time to final report?
• Crypto wallet through a bridge and into a mixer — where does Maltego's trail go cold?
• Time to train a new analyst to produce a complete investigation in Maltego vs. Orion?
• Maltego shows a graph of connections — who decides what that graph means? Is there a reasoning layer?
Manual
Every pivot requires analyst action; Orion automates investigation chains
Shallow
Maltego crypto depth vs. TRM BLOCKINT native integration in Orion
10x
Investigation speed advantage in crypto/cyber use cases vs. manual Maltego
🗺️ Orion Partner Ecosystem DATA INTELLIGENCE NETWORK · SALES DIFFERENTIATION

Orion is the AI investigation orchestration layer — not just a blockchain tool. It fuses 13+ proprietary and partner intelligence sources in a single agentic workflow. When a prospect asks "how is this different from Chainalysis?", this is your answer. Use the partner names below in every pitch — they signal that TRM has integrations Chainalysis cannot match.

⚡ ORION
AI Investigation Orchestration · Co-Case Agent · NLQ Interface
TRM CORE — Proprietary (Nobody Else Has This)
⛓️
TRM BLOCKINT
30+ chains · 1T+ transactions

7 years of proprietary attribution. The largest crypto intelligence dataset in the market. In a pitch: "Chainalysis has Bitcoin and Ethereum well covered. We have 30+ chains labelled — including all the chains DPRK uses to launder funds."

🏷️
TRM Attribution Engine
1,000+ threat actor profiles

Glass-box attribution — every label has evidence. Exchange, mixer, darknet, ransomware group labeling. In a pitch: "We don't just say 'risky.' We tell you it's LockBit's wallet and show you the proof trail."

🛡️
TRM Sanctions Intelligence
OFAC / UN / EU / UK · <10min update

Updated under 10 minutes of OFAC designation. Covers SDN, sectoral sanctions, crypto-specific. In a pitch: "During the 2022 Lazarus designation, TRM customers got alerts before competitors' manual update cycles fired."

🧠
Co-Case Agent
AI investigation orchestration

Reads 200+ intelligence sources simultaneously. Answers investigation queries in plain English. Generates SAR narratives and investigation reports. This is the Chainalysis gap: "Chainalysis gives you the data. Co-Case Agent does the reasoning."

DARK WEB INTELLIGENCE — Live in Orion (Chainalysis Has None of This)
🔥
Flashpoint
Dark web forums · Ransomware intel

Real-time dark web intelligence — ransomware group chatter, initial access broker listings, darknet marketplace activity, threat actor comms. Fused into Orion NLQ queries. Pitch angle for LE: "When LockBit posts a new victim, your investigator sees it in Orion before it's public."
Pitch angle for FinServ: "Your TPCRM team can query dark web chatter about your counterparties directly in the investigation workflow."

🔑
Hudson Rock
Infostealer · Credential intel

Infostealer credential data — compromised accounts, initial access paths, machine fingerprints linked to threat actors. When a suspect's wallet connects to a Hudson Rock infostealer cluster, Orion surfaces that link automatically. Use case: Linking a ransomware payment to the initial access broker who sold the network access.

🌐
SpiderFoot / OSINT
Web intelligence fusion

Open-source intelligence fusion across websites, social media, domain registrations, and infrastructure. Connects blockchain addresses to real-world identities through OSINT chains. Demo moment: Query a ransomware wallet in Orion → Co-Case Agent links the receiving address to a Telegram username found in a dark web forum through OSINT chain.

VERIFICATION & COMPLIANCE PARTNERS
📋
Dow Jones Risk & Compliance
PEP · Adverse media · Sanctions

PEP screening, adverse media, and global sanctions data fused into Orion entity profiles. Connects blockchain addresses to real-world legal entities. Key for FinServ: When a wallet is linked to an entity, Orion surfaces the Dow Jones risk profile alongside the blockchain trace — SAR-ready in one workflow.

🏢
Dun & Bradstreet
Corporate registry · Ownership

Corporate registry and beneficial ownership data. Traces wallets through shell company chains to ultimate beneficial owners. Key for sanctions evasion cases: The gap between "this wallet belongs to Shell Co X" and "Shell Co X is ultimately owned by a sanctioned individual" — Orion + D&B closes that gap automatically.

💱
Exchange KYC Network
LE exchange request support

Partnership with 100+ exchanges for KYC data sharing with LE under legal process. Orion identifies which exchange holds the target funds and generates the legal request template. LE pitch: "Orion shows you who owns the wallet AND generates the exchange legal request — in the same interface."

How to Use This in Pitches
Against Chainalysis objection:
"Chainalysis gives you blockchain data — excellent at that. Orion gives you blockchain + Flashpoint dark web + Hudson Rock infostealer + entity registry in a single AI query. Ask them: can Chainalysis Reactor answer 'Show me all dark web forum posts by the actor who owns this wallet'? No. We can."
For LE discovery:
"What happens when your investigator traces a wallet to a Tornado Cash mixer or a DEX? They're stuck — the blockchain ends. Orion continues: Co-Case Agent searches Flashpoint dark web, SpiderFoot OSINT, and Hudson Rock at the same time and often finds the actor through a different vector."
🎙️Expert Interviews & MediaSALES AMMUNITION
Key interviews and media that validate Orion's positioning. Use quotes in discovery and pitch conversations.

🎤Custom Pitches by Vertical
Cyber-first pitch frameworks for every vertical. Orion is an AI-native cyber investigation platform — crypto/blockchain tracing is one capability, not the whole story.
🏛️
Government / Law Enforcement
LEAD WITH:

AI-native investigation that replaces 3-4 fragmented tools. Threat actor attribution in seconds, not weeks. Dark web intelligence from 15+ marketplaces and 200+ Telegram channels. Cross-border investigation coordination.

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Cryptocurrency tracing for financial crime, DPRK attribution for national security, sanctions evasion monitoring.

OPENING STAT:

"Your analysts shouldn't need 5 tools to investigate one threat actor."

🏦
Financial Services
LEAD WITH:

Proactive dark web monitoring for threats targeting your institution. AI-powered threat actor attribution for incident response. Regulatory compliance acceleration (SEC disclosure, DORA, NIS2).

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Crypto fraud investigation, VASP compliance, blockchain tracing for institutions with digital asset exposure.

OPENING STAT:

"How quickly can your team attribute a cyber incident to a specific threat group? Orion does it in seconds."

🏥
Healthcare
LEAD WITH:

Ransomware group attribution and dark web extortion monitoring. PHI exposure detection across dark web marketplaces. HIPAA breach response acceleration — reduce investigation time from weeks to hours.

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Ransom payment tracing (crypto), supply chain vendor compromise monitoring.

OPENING STAT:

"Healthcare is hit by ransomware every 2 seconds. When it's your turn, how fast can you attribute the attack?"

🏢
Enterprise / Tech / CISO
LEAD WITH:

Proactive threat intelligence — know when your org is being discussed on the dark web. Credential exposure monitoring, executive impersonation detection, supply chain compromise intelligence.

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Crypto-related threats for companies with Web3/DeFi exposure, competitor intelligence gathering.

OPENING STAT:

"How do you know if your company is being discussed on the dark web right now? Orion tells you in real-time."

⚡
Critical Infrastructure / Energy / OT
LEAD WITH:

Nation-state APT attribution and infrastructure mapping. OT/ICS threat monitoring on dark web forums. Regulatory compliance (NIS2, SOCI Act, CIRCIA) with investigation capability.

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Ransomware financial tracing for ransom payment analysis, sanctions compliance for state-affiliated threat actors.

OPENING STAT:

"Nation-state actors are actively targeting your sector. Orion attributes the attack and maps their infrastructure before they strike."

🛡️
Insurance / Cyber Underwriting
LEAD WITH:

Pre-bind risk assessment using dark web intelligence. Claims investigation acceleration with AI-powered threat actor attribution. Portfolio monitoring for emerging threat exposure.

ADD WHEN RELEVANT:

Crypto fraud claims investigation, ransomware payment tracing for recovery analysis.

OPENING STAT:

"What if you could see a policyholder's dark web exposure before binding the policy?"

🚀 Easy Button — Instant Sales Playbook 48 MARKETS · 10 SEGMENTS

Select a country and customer type. Get your full go-to-market play in 10 seconds.

Static data — last updated at app build time
🚀
Select a country and customer type above
Your complete go-to-market playbook generates instantly
🔎Private Sector Discovery Questions10 VERTICALS · 4-PHASE FRAMEWORK
Consultative discovery framework for private sector prospects. Four phases per vertical: Current State → Pain & Impact → Orion Fit → Process & Budget. Always listen for trigger words: manual, slow, multiple tools, compliance burden, attribution, dark web, cross-chain.
🏦
Financial Services — Tier 1 Banks
JP Morgan · BofA · Citi · HSBC · Deutsche Bank · Barclays
🔭 CURRENT STATE

Walk me through your current workflow when a customer reports a crypto-related fraud or AML alert — from first flag to case closure. How many tools does one case touch?

When your AML team encounters a suspicious cross-chain transaction, what's the investigation process? Where does the trail typically go cold?

How does your crypto compliance capability compare to where regulators expect you to be right now?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

How many SAR filings per month involve crypto, and what percentage are inconclusive due to tracing limitations? What's the regulatory risk of those inconclusives?

What's your current mean time-to-attribution on a complex crypto fraud case? What would a 70% reduction in that time mean operationally?

Have you had any regulator exams cite gaps in your crypto AML capability? What did those findings cost in remediation or fines?

⚡ ORION FIT

If you could trace ransomware payments across 7 chains through 3 mixers to an exchange account in under 30 seconds — how would that change your IR and SAR process?

Orion unifies blockchain tracing, dark web monitoring, and OSINT into a single investigation. What would eliminating 3–4 tool switches per case mean for your team's throughput?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is your crypto investigation capability funded out of AML/compliance, cyber, or fraud? Who owns the budget decision — CISO, Chief Compliance Officer, or Head of Fraud?

How are you currently handling Travel Rule compliance for crypto transfers? If there's a gap, who is accountable for closing it?

🔐
Crypto Exchange / VASP
Coinbase · Binance · Kraken · Fireblocks · BitGo · Revolut
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How does your compliance team currently screen for sanctions exposure — real-time or batch? What's your false positive rate, and how much analyst time does it consume?

When your platform receives a law enforcement subpoena or production order for transaction data, how long does it take to fulfill? What's the bottleneck?

What's your current toolset for investigating suspicious accounts — do you have cross-chain tracing capability or are you limited to your own chain?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When was the last time you discovered indirect exposure to a sanctioned entity after the transaction had already settled? What was the remediation cost?

How are you managing DPRK-linked wallet exposure with 30+ blockchains in play? What happens when Lazarus moves funds through a chain you don't currently monitor?

With FATF Travel Rule enforcement accelerating globally, what gaps exist in your current cross-chain compliance stack?

⚡ ORION FIT

What if you could cross-reference dark web threat intel — stealer logs, compromised accounts, chatter — with on-chain activity for proactive threat detection before a bad actor hits your platform?

Orion automatically generates investigation narratives for law enforcement requests — cutting fulfillment time from days to hours. What would that mean for your legal team's capacity?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is your crypto compliance tool selection owned by your CISO, Chief Compliance Officer, or Head of Financial Crime? What does the evaluation process look like?

🛡️
Cyber Insurance
AIG · Chubb · Zurich · Munich Re · Coalition · At-Bay
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How do you currently assess crypto-related cyber risk for policy underwriting? What data sources inform your risk models for ransomware exposure?

When a ransomware claim comes in involving crypto payments, how does your claims team currently investigate the incident and validate the loss?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What percentage of ransomware claims involve cryptocurrency payments your team can't independently trace? How does that affect claims settlement accuracy?

When a claimant says they paid $2M in Bitcoin — can you independently verify that, or do you have to take their word for it?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion lets you independently trace ransom payments on-chain, verify claimed losses, and generate court-admissible investigation reports. How would that change your claims investigation process?

What if you could add blockchain exposure screening to your underwriting risk models — flagging policyholders with dark web credential exposure before a breach occurs?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Does this budget sit with your Claims team, your Cyber Underwriting group, or shared? Who is the economic buyer for investigation tooling?

🏥
Healthcare
Hospital Systems · Health Insurers · Pharma · MedTech · MCOs
🔭 CURRENT STATE

Walk me through your incident response when ransomware hits a clinical system. What's your current time from detection to attribution, and how many tools does that involve?

When you're negotiating with or investigating a ransomware threat actor, what visibility do you have into their wallet infrastructure and prior victims?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What's the cost per hour of clinical downtime during a ransomware event at your largest facility? How does that compare to the cost of better prevention and faster response?

For HIPAA breach notifications, how confident are you in your ability to provide accurate attribution and scope to regulators under the 72-hour clock?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can identify the ransomware group from the first payment trace, surface their dark web infrastructure, and generate the breach notification narrative automatically. What would that mean for your IR team's speed?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Does your cyber investigation capability fall under the CISO, Legal/Compliance, or your managed security provider? Who has authority to procure investigation tooling?

⚡
Critical Infrastructure / Energy / OT
Utilities · Oil & Gas · Manufacturing · Defense Contractors
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How do you currently monitor for nation-state threats targeting your OT/ICS environment? When you get an alert, what's the process for determining if it's state-sponsored?

Do you have visibility into dark web chatter about your infrastructure — threat actor reconnaissance, leaked credentials for OT systems, pre-attack indicators?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When Sandworm or Volt Typhoon targets organizations like yours, how quickly can you determine if you're being pre-positioned and by whom? What's the gap in your current attribution capability?

Under CIRCIA, you have 72 hours to report a cyber incident. Can your current toolset generate attribution and scope detail fast enough to meet that obligation?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion maps the full picture — blockchain, dark web, OSINT, infrastructure — in a single view. For a nation-state incident, that means attribution in hours not weeks. How does that change your board-level risk reporting?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is cyber investigation funded out of your CISO budget, OT security budget, or enterprise risk? Are there NERC CIP or sector-specific compliance drivers that create budget urgency?

🖥️
Technology / SaaS / Cloud
Hyperscalers · SaaS Platforms · Cloud Providers · ISPs
🔭 CURRENT STATE

When your platform is abused for crypto fraud, ransomware C2, or dark web market hosting — how do you currently detect it and what's your investigation process?

How does your Trust & Safety or abuse team currently identify actors using your infrastructure for illicit crypto activity?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When you receive a law enforcement legal process (subpoena, MLAT, emergency disclosure), how long does it take to fulfill? What's the bottleneck — data access, investigation, or documentation?

What happens to your platform reputation and customer trust when you're publicly linked to hosting illicit infrastructure — ransomware C2, dark web markets, crypto mixers?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion lets your abuse team cross-reference dark web actor profiles with on-platform behavior and crypto infrastructure — identifying bad actors before incidents escalate. How valuable is proactive detection vs reactive investigation?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Does this sit with your CISO, Trust & Safety lead, Legal, or your Government Affairs team? Who handles law enforcement cooperation and tooling decisions?

🎰
Gaming / iGaming / NFT
Online Casinos · Sports Betting · NFT Marketplaces · Play-to-Earn
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How do you currently screen crypto deposits and withdrawals for sanctions and money laundering? Are you monitoring for wash trading and NFT-based money laundering?

When regulators audit your AML program for crypto exposure, what evidence can you produce for your transaction monitoring methodology?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

With FATF guidance now explicitly calling out gaming and NFT platforms as high-risk, what's your exposure if a regulator determines your current AML program isn't fit for purpose?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion provides the complete audit trail — on-chain tracing, dark web actor profiles, automated risk scoring — that regulators expect to see. What would that mean for your next compliance exam?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is this a Compliance budget, a Fraud budget, or shared? Are there specific licensing jurisdictions (Malta, Gibraltar, Curaçao) creating compliance urgency right now?

🔬
DFIR / Incident Response Firms
Mandiant · CrowdStrike · Palo Alto Unit 42 · Kroll · Kivu
🔭 CURRENT STATE

When your IR team responds to a ransomware engagement, how do you currently handle the crypto payment tracing component? Do you have in-house blockchain capability or do you outsource it?

On a complex multi-chain ransom payment case, what's your current investigation workflow? How many tools, and how long?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When a client needs attribution in 24 hours to support a law enforcement referral or insurance claim, can your current toolset deliver? Where does it fall short?

What's the revenue impact when you have to turn away or subcontract crypto-heavy ransomware cases because of tooling gaps?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion cuts IR crypto investigation from days to hours — blockchain tracing, dark web actor profiles, auto-generated investigation reports, all in one platform. How does that change your incident response SLA?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is tooling licensed per engagement, per analyst, or enterprise? Who makes the call — your practice lead, CISO, or procurement? Are you looking at this for a specific client engagement or your entire IR practice?

🏛️
Asset Management / Hedge Funds / Private Equity
BlackRock · Fidelity · Citadel · a16z Crypto · Galaxy Digital
🔭 CURRENT STATE

As your firm allocates to digital assets, how do you screen counterparties and custodians for sanctions, AML, and dark web exposure? What's your due diligence process?

When you invest in a crypto project or protocol, how do you assess the risk of the founding team's on-chain history or dark web presence?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What's your exposure if an LP or regulator determines you failed to conduct adequate AML due diligence on a crypto asset that later turns out to be sanctioned or illicit?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion provides institutional-grade due diligence — on-chain history, dark web mentions, sanctions exposure, counterparty risk scoring — in minutes. How does that fit into your existing diligence workflow?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Does this budget sit with Compliance, Risk, or your Digital Assets team? Is this a point-in-time diligence purchase or an ongoing monitoring subscription?

🌐
Telecom / ISP / MSP
AT&T · Verizon · BT · Deutsche Telekom · NTT · Managed Security Providers
🔭 CURRENT STATE

When your network is used for crypto mining malware, botnet C2, or dark web relay infrastructure — how quickly do you detect it and what's your investigation process?

For your managed security clients, how are you currently providing crypto threat intelligence and dark web monitoring as part of your SOC offering?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What's your liability exposure when your network is used as infrastructure for a major ransomware attack and you couldn't detect it in time? How does that affect client contracts?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can be embedded as a managed service add-on — dark web monitoring, crypto threat intel, investigation support — for your enterprise clients. What's the revenue opportunity if you could add that capability to your SOC portfolio?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is this an internal security investment or are you evaluating as a value-add service to resell? Who owns the decision — CISO, Product, or your Managed Services P&L?

🏥
Healthcare & Life Sciences
Mayo · Cleveland Clinic · UnitedHealth · CVS Health · Humana · Cigna
🔭 CURRENT STATE

Walk me through what happens when your security team identifies a ransomware IOC tied to a healthcare sector group — how quickly can you determine if patient data has been exfiltrated, and through which channel?

When a vendor or third-party system is compromised, how do you trace lateral movement that may have reached billing or payment systems with crypto payment options for ransomware?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

Post-ransomware breach (like the Change Healthcare $22M payment in 2024) — what's your process for determining if a ransom payment is to a sanctioned entity? What's the liability exposure if you pay without checking?

HIPAA requires breach notification within 60 days. What part of that timeline is currently eaten by manual investigation work? What would accelerating it mean for regulatory exposure?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can identify ransomware gang identity, confirm OFAC status, and trace payment destination in under 5 minutes — before you make any payment decision. Would that change how you handle active incidents?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is your cyber budget centralized under the CISO, or split between IT security and compliance? With OCR enforcement of HIPAA at record levels, has compliance driven new budget conversations for investigation tools?

⚡
Critical Infrastructure — Energy & Utilities
Duke Energy · Con Edison · National Grid · Shell · BP · Schneider Electric
🔭 CURRENT STATE

After the Colonial Pipeline incident, how has your team's mandate for OT/ICS investigation changed? When a ransom demand arrives for an ICS environment, what's your current workflow from initial detection to response decision?

Volt Typhoon and similar groups are pre-positioning in critical infrastructure for disruption, not immediate ransomware. How does your threat intelligence program currently track whether nation-state actors have crypto-funded access brokers selling into your sector?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

CIRCIA mandates 72-hour reporting to CISA for critical infrastructure cyber incidents. What part of your current process makes that timeline difficult — and how much is consumed by manual threat actor attribution?

When ransomware hits OT systems (as it did with Oldsmar water treatment, Colonial Pipeline), the downstream cost is measured in operational disruption, not just IT recovery. How does that change your willingness to invest in tools that accelerate attribution and payment risk assessment?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion gives you actor identity, ransom payment history, sanctions status, and negotiation intelligence from dark web monitoring in under 10 minutes — before CISA notification deadline starts to pressure your IR team. Does that close a gap?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is cyber budget for OT investigation tools managed separately from IT security? Post-CIRCIA, have you seen budget for incident response and investigation tooling increase at the executive or board level?

🎮
Gaming, iGaming & NFT Platforms
Activision · EA · DraftKings · FanDuel · OpenSea · Magic Eden · Sorare
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How does your platform currently detect when in-game currency or NFT assets are being used for money laundering — specifically layering crypto proceeds through virtual asset purchases and resales?

When you identify suspicious account activity or chargeback fraud involving crypto — what's the investigation workflow? How long does it take to connect a wallet to an account identity?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

FATF guidance now includes virtual asset game platforms under AML obligations in several jurisdictions. Are you currently exposed on VASP compliance requirements, and if regulators audited your AML program for virtual assets, where would the gaps be?

Account takeover via stealer malware is endemic to gaming platforms — credentials from 30M+ compromised accounts are indexed and sold daily. How does your fraud team currently detect ATO driven by dark web credential markets?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can cross-reference your suspicious account against 30M+ dark web stealer logs in real time — identifying ATO via credential compromise before your fraud team discovers it through chargebacks. What would that mean for your dispute rate?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Does your fraud and AML budget sit under the CFO, CISO, or a separate financial crime function? As VASP regulation expands to gaming, where is compliance investment being made today?

🔗
Technology — Cloud & SaaS Platforms
AWS · Microsoft · Google · Cloudflare · Salesforce · ServiceNow · Okta
🔭 CURRENT STATE

When your platform is used as infrastructure by a threat actor — as hosting for C2, phishing sites, or laundering operations — what's your process from abuse report to takedown? How do you investigate the crypto financial layer funding those campaigns?

When ransomware groups use your cloud infrastructure for staging or C2 (as in the AWS/Azure abuse cases), how quickly can your abuse team identify the financial network behind the group and notify LE?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

Reputational and regulatory risk from hosting criminal infrastructure is growing. How does your Trust & Safety team currently qualify whether an account is linked to a known threat actor or sanctioned entity before takedown?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can cross-reference suspicious infrastructure against criminal actor profiles — wallet clusters, dark web forum mentions, known C2 indicators — in a single query. What would cutting your abuse investigation time from days to hours mean for your compliance posture?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is your Trust & Safety and fraud investigation capability owned by the CISO, Legal/Compliance, or a dedicated T&S function? How is investigation tooling budget decided across those teams?

🔍Public Sector Discovery Questions8 AGENCY TYPES · MISSION-FIRST FRAMEWORK
Discovery questions for government, law enforcement, and intelligence buyers. Lead with mission impact, not product features. Listen for: caseload, attribution speed, evidence admissibility, multi-agency coordination, procurement constraints, existing tool contracts.
🏛️
Federal Law Enforcement
FBI · Secret Service · IRS-CI · HSI · DEA · ATF · USMS
🔭 CURRENT STATE

What tools do your cyber investigators currently use for attribution? How many separate platforms does one case touch from initial alert to prosecution referral?

How does your team currently handle cases where crypto payments cross 5+ blockchains and pass through mixers? At what point does the investigation stall?

How much of your investigative time is spent on manual data aggregation vs actual analysis? What's the ratio for a complex case?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What's your current backlog of crypto-enabled cases — ransomware, darknet markets, DPRK heists — that are stalled due to attribution gaps? What's the case value sitting in that backlog?

When you identify a suspect but can't produce a legally defensible financial trail, what happens to that prosecution? How often does that occur?

How does your current toolset handle dark web actor profiles, Telegram channel monitoring, and forum scraping in non-English languages?

⚡ ORION FIT

If you could reduce time-to-attribution from weeks to minutes on a complex cross-chain case, what would that mean for your caseload capacity and prosecution rate?

Orion auto-generates court-admissible investigation narratives with sourced evidence. How critical is that documentation requirement for your current cases?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is this funded through your Unit/Division budget, an ILEA grant, or a GPC purchase? What's the procurement vehicle — SEWP, GSA Schedule, or a direct sole-source?

Who needs to approve the tool evaluation — your AUSA liaison, your division chief, or CIO? What's the minimum evaluation period required?

👮
State & Local Law Enforcement
State Police · DA Offices · Fusion Centers · HIDTA · RISS
🔭 CURRENT STATE

Does your agency have dedicated cyber investigators, or do traditional detectives handle crypto-related digital evidence? What's the training gap?

When a victim reports a crypto-enabled fraud or romance scam, how much of the financial trail can your team actually follow? Where does it hit a wall?

Are you currently working with a fusion center or HIDTA task force for crypto cases? How do you currently share intelligence and case data across agencies?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

How many crypto-related cases from the past 12 months resulted in charges vs. were shelved due to inability to trace the financial flow? What's the attrition rate?

With pig butchering scams and darknet drug trafficking hitting your jurisdiction, how prepared is your team to meet victim expectations and prosecute successfully?

⚡ ORION FIT

What if a detective with no crypto background could trace illicit funds across chains, produce a prosecution-ready report, and close a case in 15 minutes? How does that change your capacity?

Orion's auto-translate capability covers 50+ languages — critical for darknet forums and Telegram channels operating in Russian, Mandarin, and Arabic. How often do language barriers stall your investigations?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

What grant programs is your agency currently drawing on — Byrne JAG, HIDTA, COPS? Could Orion be justified as an investigative capability investment under those grants?

🛡️
Defense & Intelligence Community
DoD · DIA · NSA · CYBERCOM · NGA · SOCOM · IC Agencies
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How does your team currently track financial flows associated with nation-state cyber operations — DPRK, Russia, Iran, China? What's the integration between your SIGINT and financial tracing capability?

When you identify threat actor infrastructure, can you trace the funding back to state sponsors using current tooling? What's missing in that attribution chain?

How are you currently monitoring dark web infrastructure for pre-attack indicators against DoD contractors, cleared facilities, or critical programs?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When Lazarus Group or APT38 executes a crypto heist to fund DPRK's weapons program, how quickly can you produce attribution-quality analysis for policy makers? What's the gap?

For adversary finance tracking — sanctioned entities, crypto wallets linked to state programs — how comprehensive is your current coverage across 30+ blockchains?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion combines DPRK financial attribution, dark web OSINT, and multi-chain tracing in a single platform deployable in air-gapped or classified environments. How does that align with your current toolset architecture?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

What contract vehicle would you use — OTA, SBIR, SEWP V, or a program-specific contract? Is there an existing IDIQ or umbrella vehicle we should be positioned on?

🌐
International Law Enforcement & Europol/Interpol
Europol EC3 · INTERPOL · NCA · AFP · BKA · Gendarmerie · RCMP
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How do you currently coordinate cross-border crypto investigations with partner agencies? What's the tool interoperability challenge — are you all using different platforms?

When a case involves entities in multiple jurisdictions with different evidence admissibility standards, how do you produce investigation output that works for all partners?

What's the current state of your agency's ability to conduct multi-language dark web monitoring — Russian, Mandarin, Arabic, Spanish forums?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

How many joint operations in the past year were delayed or compromised by incompatible investigation platforms across member states? What's the operational cost?

When a ransomware operator is identified in Eastern Europe but the victim and the payments are in 5 different countries, how does your current toolset handle that attribution?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion supports shared investigation workspaces for multi-agency operations with role-based access. How would a common investigation platform change your joint operation coordination?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is procurement through an EU Framework Agreement, national procurement, or Europol's own contracting authority? Who needs to sign off — your Head of Cyber, budget officer, or a multi-agency committee?

🏦
Financial Intelligence Units (FIUs)
FinCEN · AUSTRAC · FINTRAC · NCIS · Tracfin · FIU-Netherlands
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How does your FIU currently process SAR filings that involve crypto — do you have in-house blockchain tracing capability or do you rely on referring agencies?

When you receive a suspicious transaction report involving crypto, what's your current analysis workflow? How long does it take from receipt to actionable intelligence product?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

What percentage of your crypto-related SAR volume results in actionable intelligence products vs. gets archived due to insufficient tracing capability? What happens to those cases?

With FATF Recommendation 16 (Travel Rule) enforcement accelerating, how prepared is your agency to assess VASP compliance and identify reporting gaps?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion lets your FIU analysts produce exchange-ready intelligence packages — on-chain evidence, actor profiles, sanction exposure — in minutes instead of weeks. How does that change your intelligence production throughput?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is tooling acquisition centralized through Treasury/Finance Ministry, or does your FIU have independent procurement authority? What's the typical approval timeline for a tool of this class?

🔬
Cybercrime Prosecutors / Legal Authorities
DOJ · USAO · CPS · NCA Legal · Eurojust · State AG Offices
🔭 CURRENT STATE

What's your current process for establishing the evidentiary foundation for crypto-related charges — forfeiture, money laundering, sanctions violations? What tool outputs are admissible in your jurisdiction?

When investigators hand off a crypto case to your office, how often is the financial evidence package sufficient to prosecute vs. needing supplemental investigation?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

How many crypto-related cases have you declined to prosecute or seen dismissed because the blockchain evidence wasn't defensible in court? What's driving those failures?

As defense counsel increasingly challenges blockchain analytics methodology, how is your office preparing expert witnesses and documentation standards?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion generates fully sourced, court-ready investigation narratives with reproducible methodology. How does that compare to the expert witness packages your investigators currently produce?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is tooling acquisition a prosecutorial budget item, shared with your investigative agency, or covered by asset forfeiture funds? Who approves technology acquisitions for your office?

🏗️
Critical Infrastructure / CISA / Sector CISOs
CISA · Sector Risk Management Agencies · National CSIRT teams
🔭 CURRENT STATE

Under CIRCIA, your sector entities now have 72-hour incident reporting obligations. What's your current capability to help them attribute and scope an incident fast enough to meet that deadline?

How are you currently tracking ransomware groups targeting your sector — their infrastructure, wallet activity, dark web communications, and victim targeting patterns?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

When a major infrastructure operator is hit and the ransom demand involves crypto, what's your current playbook? Who traces the payment and what's the timeline to attribution?

What's the gap in your current sector threat intelligence — are you seeing threats but unable to produce actionable attribution for sector partners in time to matter?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion can be deployed as a sector-level investigation platform — shared across your sector entities, with investigation collaboration, threat actor tracking, and unified dark web monitoring. How does that fit your information sharing architecture?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is this funded through DHS grants, sector ISAC budgets, or a direct CISA program? What's the procurement mechanism — GSA, SEWP, or a direct award?

🌏
APAC Government & LE
Singapore SPF · AFP · KNPA Korea · NPA Japan · POLRI Indonesia · RTP Thailand
🔭 CURRENT STATE

How does your agency currently handle cross-border crypto investigations — particularly cases involving exchanges in Singapore, Hong Kong, or offshore jurisdictions?

What's your current capability for investigating DPRK-linked crypto activity targeting regional banks and exchanges? Is there dedicated tooling for that threat vector?

💥 PAIN & IMPACT

With pig butchering (sha zhu pan) scams devastating APAC victims and moving billions through regional exchanges, what's your current investigation bandwidth? What cases are falling through?

How are you managing multi-language investigation needs — Mandarin, Bahasa, Thai, Korean — for dark web and Telegram-based criminal networks?

⚡ ORION FIT

Orion's auto-translation (50+ languages) and APAC exchange coverage makes it uniquely suited for regional crypto crime. How would that capability change your cross-border coordination with regional partners?

💼 PROCESS & BUDGET

Is procurement handled through your Ministry of Home Affairs, Ministry of Finance, or a technology procurement directorate? What's the typical evaluation period and approval chain?

🌍Country Intelligence Profiles30 KEY MARKETS · UPDATED Q2 2026
Each profile covers: Key Regulations · Active Threats · Primary Agencies · Competitor Landscape · Orion Opportunity · Budget Cycle · Procurement Notes · Active TRM Deals/POCs. Use to tailor pitch, select relevant threat intel, and map to buyer personas.
TIER 1 — CORE MARKETS (Active Deals / POCs)
🇺🇸
United States
TAM: $1.2B+ · Opportunity: CRITICAL
ACTIVE

Regs: CIRCIA (72hr reporting), FinCEN Travel Rule, OFAC sanctions, SEC cyber rules, CISA directives, Executive Order 14028

Top Threats: DPRK Lazarus/APT38 crypto heists ($1.5B/yr), LockBit 4.0/ALPHV ransomware, Chinese APT (Volt Typhoon, Salt Typhoon critical infra), Russian Sandworm, BEC fraud

Key Agencies: FBI Cyber/CCIU · IRS-CI NCITA · HSI Cyber · DEA SOS · USSS CID · DHS CISA · NSA · CYBERCOM · FinCEN · SEC

Competitors: Chainalysis (FBI, DEA standard) · TRM Forensics (banks, some LE) · Elliptic (compliance) · Palantir (IC) · i2/IBM (LE intel)

Orion Status: 35+ active opportunities · FBI, IRS-CI, HSI, DEA, NSA, CYBERCOM, SEC in pipeline · Coinbase, Binance, Kraken deals active

Budget Cycle: Federal FY Oct 1 – Sep 30 · Supplemental requests Apr–Jun · NDAA funding cycle for defense · DHS appropriations separate from IC community

Procurement Vehicles: GSA MAS 70 · DHS EAGLE II · CIO-SP3 · SEWP V · OTA agreements for IC · IDIQ task orders for agency-specific buys · Small Business set-aside consideration

Key Buying Triggers 2026: DPRK $1.5B Bybit heist response · CIRCIA rulemaking finalization · FBI blockchain analytics recompete · IRS-CI NCITA program expansion · DHS CISA cyber budget +18%

🇬🇧
United Kingdom
TAM: $180M+ · Opportunity: CRITICAL
POC ACTIVE

Regs: Economic Crime Act 2023, Computer Misuse Act, FCA PS23/4 crypto rules, NIS2 (via UK NIS), DCMS Cyber Bill, Money Laundering Regulations

Top Threats: NHS ransomware (ALPHV/LockBit NHS attack 2024), Russian GRU/SVR espionage, DPRK crypto heists, IAB ecosystem, darknet drug trafficking (UK #2 globally)

Key Agencies: NCA NCCU · Met Police Cyber Crime · GCHQ/NCSC · HMRC CSTF · FCA · SOCA successor units · UKSA · Regional Cyber Crime Units

Competitors: i2/IBM Analyst Notebook (GCHQ/NCA standard) · Palantir (Met evaluation) · Chainalysis (FCA-regulated firms) · TRM Compliance (banks)

Orion Status: NCA POC complete (£500K) · Met Police pilot starting Apr 2026 (8-week paid) · HSBC, Barclays pipeline

Budget Cycle: UK FY Apr 1 – Mar 31 · Spending Review every 3–5 years · Home Office/NCA annual allocation · NCSC part of GCHQ budget (classified)

Procurement Vehicles: Crown Commercial Service G-Cloud · Crown Commercial Service RM6161 · DSTL OTA for GCHQ/NCSC · OJEU/Find a Tender Service · Police Digital Service frameworks

Key Buying Triggers 2026: Economic Crime Act enforcement acceleration · NCA NCCU budget increase post-LockBit takedown · Met Police crypto crime unit expansion · FCA PS23/4 VASP compliance deadline

🇦🇺
Australia
TAM: $95M · Opportunity: HIGH
ACTIVE

Regs: SOCI Act 2018 (amended 2022), AML/CTF Act Tranche 2 (2026 enforcement), AUSTRAC VASP obligations, ASD Essential Eight, Privacy Act reform 2024

Top Threats: Chinese espionage (APT40/Hafnium targeting AUKUS programs), healthcare ransomware (Medibank breach 2022), crypto compliance failures, BEC fraud, DPRK targeting APAC exchanges

Key Agencies: AFP Cybercrime · AUSTRAC · ASD/ACSC · ASIO · State Police Cyber Units · ACIC

Competitors: i2/IBM Analyst Notebook (AFP standard) · Palantir (DSTO evaluation) · TRM Compliance (exchanges) · NICE Actimize (banks)

Orion Status: AUSTRAC active pipeline · AFP evaluation · AUD RFQ expected Q3 2026 ($8-25M) · Singapore SPF deal references APAC expansion

🇸🇬
Singapore
TAM: $70M · Opportunity: HIGH
ACTIVE

Regs: MAS PSA Digital Tokens, MAS MAS TRM Guidelines, FATF Asia/Pacific compliance, Cybersecurity Act 2018, MHA Digital Crimes framework, AML VASP licensing (MAS regulated)

Top Threats: Pig butchering scams (Singapore #1 APAC victim 2024, $650M+ losses), DPRK targeting SG exchanges, Chinese APT targeting financial sector, money laundering via VASPs

Key Agencies: SPF Cybercrime Command · MAS · CAD (Commercial Affairs Dept) · ISD · CSA (Cyber Security Agency) · DSTA

Competitors: Chainalysis (SGX-listed exchange compliance) · TRM Compliance (banks) · Elliptic (MAS-regulated VASPs)

Orion Status: SPF active pipeline · MAS evaluation · DSTA $2M deal in progress · Merkle/SG FinTech sector active

🇮🇱
Israel
TAM: $60M · Opportunity: HIGH
ACTIVE

Regs: INCD Cyber Defense Directive, Bank of Israel AML requirements, VASP registration (ISA), Prohibition on Money Laundering Law, NIS2-equivalent national framework

Top Threats: Iranian APT (Charming Kitten, Tortoiseshell) targeting tech sector, Hezbollah-linked financial networks, Hamas crypto fundraising, Hamas cyberattacks post-Oct 2023, state-sponsored disinformation

Key Agencies: INCD · Shin Bet (ISA) · Mossad (technical) · Israel National Police Lahav 433 · Bank of Israel · ISA (financial regulator)

Competitors: Israeli-made OSINT tools (NSO-adjacent) · Chainalysis (banks) · Native Israeli solutions (Sygnia, CyberArk ecosystem)

Orion Status: Shamir deal ($1.19M) · Israel National Police evaluation · Tal Croitoru leading · Active Hamas/Iran financial tracing use cases

🇩🇪
Germany
TAM: $130M · Opportunity: HIGH
PIPELINE

Regs: NIS2 Umsetzungsgesetz (30,000+ entities from 2024), BSI IT-Grundschutz, BaFin MiCAR enforcement, GwG AML Act, DORA (financial sector), EU AI Act

Top Threats: Russian APT (Sandworm/APT28 Bundestag attacks, ongoing), Mittelstand ransomware epidemic, industrial espionage (Chinese APT targeting Baden-Württemberg), BEC targeting German banks

Key Agencies: BKA Cybercrime Division SO4 · BSI · BND (signals) · BfV · BaFin Digital Assets · ZIT Frankfurt (crypto prosecution) · LKA units

TIER 2 — HIGH-GROWTH MARKETS (Active Pursuit)
🇫🇷
France
TAM: $120M · Opportunity: HIGH

Regs: LPM 2024-2030 (€3B cybersecurity investment), NIS2, RGPD/CNIL, MiCAR, AML6D, AMF Virtual Assets framework

Top Threats: Russian state-sponsored attacks on OIV (operators of vital importance), hospital ransomware (CHU Versailles 2022), darknet drug trafficking (France #1 EU consumer), election interference (RU disinformation), DPRK crypto

Key Agencies: ANSSI · DGSI (counterintel) · Gendarmerie C3N (cybercrime) · OCLCTIC (police cyber) · Tracfin (FIU) · AMF · Parquet National Financier

Competitors: i2/IBM (Gendarmerie/DGSI standard) · TRM Compliance (BNP Paribas, Société Générale) · Elliptic (banks)

🇦🇪
UAE
TAM: $85M · Opportunity: HIGH

Regs: Cybercrime Law 34/2021, NESA CII Framework, CBUAE Virtual Assets Regulation, VARA licensing (Dubai), ADGM/DIFC VASP rules, FATF greylisting exit requirements
